items.title: LongCat 2.0 pushes frontier-scale open MoE
items.oneliner: LongCat 2.0 trained a 1.6T MoE with 48B active on 35T tokens, proving frontier scale is no longer Nvidia-only.

items.title: Ornith makes coding agents self-improving
items.oneliner: Ornith's 397B MoE uses reinforcement learning to improve both code and scaffolds, raising the bar for open coding agents.

items.title: Meta decodes typed sentences from brain scans
items.oneliner: Meta says Brain2Qwerty hit 61% average word accuracy from non-invasive scans, making brain-to-text feel less speculative.

items.title: One million passport records were exposed
items.oneliner: More than one million passport records leaked from cannabis club software, turning weak KYC storage into an identity-theft factory.

items.title: The Supreme Court limits geofence warrants
items.oneliner: The Supreme Court put geofence warrants under Fourth Amendment scrutiny, raising the compliance bar for location-data products.

items.title: Europe's ID wallets may entrench duopolies
items.oneliner: European digital ID wallets lean on Google and Apple attestation, undermining sovereignty with infrastructure Europe does not control.

items.title: Apple's Neural Engine gets reverse-engineered
items.oneliner: A new ANE paper maps Apple chips from A11 to M5, giving ML engineers rare performance guidance beyond Core ML marketing.

items.title: ZLUDA 6 broadens CUDA portability
items.oneliner: ZLUDA 6 runs more unmodified CUDA workloads on non-Nvidia GPUs, which could weaken CUDA lock-in at the edges.

items.title: Cursor puts cloud coding agents on phones
items.oneliner: Cursor's iOS app lets developers launch agents and review PR diffs from mobile, pushing coding workflows beyond the desktop.

items.title: AI is pressuring the billable hour
items.oneliner: Consulting firms are shifting from hourly billing toward fixed and outcome-based pricing as AI compresses visible labor.
